programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: All you need in the kitchen for your precision slicing and dicing of fruits and vegetables. Including three cutting inserts, a finger protector, blade holder and a free docking station.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Steve launches an investigation into suspicions of corruption among his fellow police officers - who respond by framing him for murder. Dick and Barry Van Dyke star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The same gang involved in the Schroeder murder kidnaps Mark's underworld friend Gus Benedict. Jack and Mark try to save him before time runs out and prove Steve's innocence, but find themselves in grave danger. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble becomes a pawn in a play for love and fortune.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A man who faked his own death returns home to his wife three years later, but when he is murdered, suspicion falls on the girlfriend he had left behind.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A white supremacist group take Alex and the employees of a TV station hostage, as revenge for Walker and Trivette arresting their leader

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A baseball star's bride-to-be is shot dead by a sniper on her wedding day, and the ensuing investigation leads Calleigh and Delko to a strip club - where they discover an exotic dancer was the intended target. Armed with this knowledge, the team learns the killer's true motive - which proves surprising

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Meanwhile, Gibbs works to clear a Navy Hospital Corpsman who has been indicted for illegally providing medical aid to victims of the crash, despite the injured insisting her techniques helped save their lives. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Agents G Callen and Sam Hanna investigate after a naval officer is killed during an LAPD shootout, with two other men who died in the gun battle being identified as members of a Mexican drug cartel. Spin-off drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects, an elite NCIS division specialising in undercover assignments. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is deputized then called upon to transport a man charged with a crime to the county seat.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan and Greevey investigate the shooting of two men in a subway. At first the crime appears to be the result of self-defence, but the detectives later discover revenge could be a possible motive

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when a mysterious, fast-spreading illness strikes children from military families. While Gibbs tries to determine the cause, Abby and Jimmy partner with the Naval Medical Research Centre in the hope of delivering a cure before the holidays. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An eight-year-old crime proves a challenge for Walker and Trivette when a decomposed body of a young Indian boy is unearthed on a building site. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. NCIS joins forces with the Department of Defence to track down the device before it is used for a large-scale attack, but Gibbs is less than pleased that he has to work with his ex-lover Hollis Mann. Meanwhile, McGee hopes he will be granted time off to attend a black-tie gala honouring his girlfriend Delilah. Guest starring Susanna Thompson (Arrow)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray

programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. Kris Marshall stars

Drama, guest starring George Cole, Roy Hudd and Jan Francis

Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves, with guest appearances by Tim Pigott-Smith, Sharon Small and Priyanga Burford

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Sicknote performs a heroic rescue for a mother and baby trapped in an attic flat. Meanwhile, Recall returns to duty now that Jamie is getting better

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Maggie returns from Thailand with her new toy boy. Bayleaf is found not guilty in court, while Jean and Sicknote continue to get along swimmingly

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Zain and Terry bend the rules over the beating of a suspected paedophile, thinking they have the backing of new boss Stuart Turner. Phil Hunter clashes with an attractive solicitor who seems set to tear his case apart, but a taste of his trademark charm weakens her resolve. Morris Leatherhead trashes a kebab shop in a bid to get back to prison. Footballers' Wives star Phina Oruche guests

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mark takes James under his wing when he promises to stay clean and Clifford's euphoric time with Louise draws to its inevitable conclusion. Faye tries to help Joseph move on from his continuing pain at the thought of his father's relationship with Jac. Hospital drama, starring Robert Powell and Luke Roberts

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lara turns down another marriage proposal from Patrick, but his involvement in a major road accident forces her to re-evaluate her views. Comfort and Fin are the first to arrive on the scene and attempt to deal with the crisis alone - leading to an act of heroism and a surprising revelation. Medical drama, starring Ian Kelsey, Christine Stephen-Daly and Ian Bleasdale

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Tony Stamp swallows his pride and takes on a second job as a cabbie to pay for his father's care home. Phil Hunter's response to the attack on brother Steve convinces the PC he's not wanted at Sun Hill and he applies for a transfer. Banter in the pub about Will's conquest of Honey Harman brings the relationship to a dramatic halt, while colleagues notice Sam is cosying up to new boy Stuart Turner

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat tells Geoff people are gossiping about them, Michelle has a job interview, Cindy flirts at the swimming pool, and David gets Bianca a bedsit - but wants it to be kept a secret

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is hoping to open the chip shop soon. Sharon learns one of Phil's big secrets, and Pauline is unhappy about Kathy marrying Phil

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Fiona springs a surprise on Pearce, and a raging warehouse blaze ensures that life will never be the same again for some members of Blue Watch. Richard Walsh stars

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew and Erica clash over the best way to treat a baby suffering from a mysterious illness, while David plays guardian angel for a couple whose leukaemia-stricken son feels hemmed in by their desire to wrap him in cotton wool. Rural medical drama, starring Gary Mavers, Saskia Wickham and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul comes into conflict with DI Dagley when an inmate he treated after a prison riot dies in police custody. The surgeon vows to get to the bottom of the case, unaware of the web of intrigue surrounding the wife of the dead man. Marty plays the good uncle as Alison goes into labour. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon, Tracey and Dorien spend a quiet Saturday evening reminiscing about their first boyfriends - then decide to track down the gentlemen in question. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana takes matters into her own hands, asking Tom for sleeping pills after falling ill and fearing the worst. Stephanie Cole stars

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Gruber finishes the paintings and the spy camera sent from London has fallen into a vineyard where the peasants are refusing to work for fear of being shot

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley is almost ready to unveil his latest creation, but his odd behavour makes the trio want to find out what lies within his shed sooner than he would prefer. Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde, Bill Owen and Gordon Wharmby star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for the cops on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. However, it's all change for the team. Last week they waved goodbye to Humphrey Goodman. No one knows how Kris Marshall's character is bowing out of the show - will he walk off into the sunset with new love Martha, or will he meet a sticky end, like his predecessor Richard Poole (Ben Miller)? What we do know is that in his place is Ardal O'Hanlon as DI Jack Mooney, who first popped up in the two-part London special. His first Caribbean case sees the reopening of a seven-year-old murder when a tourist provides an alibi for the woman convicted of the crime

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo help reorganise the seed museum owned by the descendants of famous botanist Edwin Pargeter - but it is not long before a man is found dead in the family's garden. In an effort to trace his killer, Rosemary involves colleagues from the Royal Horticultural Society at Kew Gardens, and soon the race is on to identify a batch of mysterious seeds before it falls into the wrong hands. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens a 20-year-old case when a camera and film belonging to a photographic lab assistant murdered in 1987 are found by chance in a Soho pub toilet. New evidence leads the detectives into the world of modelling and to the door of a professional footballer. Meanwhile, Brian's erratic behaviour takes an alarming turn, and Gerry is less than happy when his daughter Emily (played by Dennis Waterman's real-life daughter Hannah) joins the team and looks to Sandra as a role model

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A pub landlady is stabbed to death and has the tip of her little finger cut off, and while Leo and Jack collect mounting evidence that her husband was responsible, Nikki remains convinced of the man's innocence. When he is charged with the crime, she must convince her colleagues the woman was the latest victim of a psychopathic serial killer, resulting in a deadly game of cat and mouse as they seek out the culprit. Christine Bottomley, Penny Downie and Christopher Fulford guest star in a feature-length episode of the forensic drama

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Norwich, Brynmawr in south Wales and Maidstone in Kent

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles examines the extraordinary rescue of a young man missing overnight after being swept out to sea on his surfboard. Plus, a local resident comes to the aid of a young woman who topples into a city-centre river during a night on the town with friends

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, where experts Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt help two teams to bag a bargain or two to take to auction. Plus, Eric uncovers the intriguing story behind a collection of postcards of the First World War era

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia's time off is interrupted alarmingly, and Laila's schooling causes conflict

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sophie Faldo, David Domoney, Frances Tophill, James Tanner and Will Kirk form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a retired couple search for a new home in rural Somerset with a budget of £700,000, and firm ideas on the vision for their perfect country home. Along the way, Jonnie masters the thrilling sport of land yachting at Brean Sands, where thanks to a prevailing wind he picks up the pace along this picturesque stretch of beach

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: The team recalls some of their favourite quirky garden rescues, including a music festival-inspired plot featuring an air-raid shelter, and a fusion of Yorkshire and Japan

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the New Forest and Southampton Water, and learn about tide milling. Items under the hammer include a mantle clock and a pocket watch

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The 2007 Christmas episode, in which the truth is out about Max and Stacey. It must have seemed like the perfect way to end a lovely Christmas dinner - the Branning and Slater clans settling down together to watch Bradley and Stacey's wedding DVD. Unfortunately, the families weren't expecting to see the blushing bride kissing her new father-in-law as he begged her to resume their illicit affair. Now, everyone has to digest this alarming revelation along with their turkey - and no doubt it's going to be wronged spouses Tanya and Bradley who will be hit the hardest

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric's operation takes place on the same day as Guy's disciplinary hearing, forcing him to choose between treatment that could his life and his need to see Guy face the consequences of his actions. Sacha's silent treatment leaves Essie convinced he knows something about Frankie's disappearance, and Chloe tries her hand at Internet dating - only for one of her matches to be admitted as a patient

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Gates is desperate to sever his ties with Jackie after being duped into covering up her crime, but he is plunged into an even more dangerous situation as Arnott and Fleming close in on his secret and is forced to choose between his marriage and his career. Crime thriller about a detective sergeant working in anti-corruption who is assigned to investigate a respected senior officer. Starring Lennie James, Martin Compston, Vicky McClure, Neil Morrissey and Gina McKee

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Alan Cumming and Nick Grimshaw are among the special guests joining Michelle for a special Pride episode. Chef Jaime Laurita drops in to bake a special pride cake, and Michelle performs a long-distance marriage ceremony for a couple in Scotland

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of reconnecting with nature. She tries giving up environmentally destructive habits like driving and using plastic packaging and going camping in the Welsh Hills, where she meets a family who have embraced the delights of living off-grid

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the murder of a military veteran by his estranged wife in 1825

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of property developments in Romford, east London, the West Midlands city of Wolverhampton and Aberdare in Wales

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A professional British cyclist is involved in a horrific collision with a race support vehicle, and two men using an accelerant to start a bonfire get more than they bargained for

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from Wadebridge in Cornwall as experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help the teams find antiques to sell on at auction.

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: The locals celebrate a big night for Iris, and Ash finds Helena to be a kindred spirit

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Musical comedian Mitch Benn, wine expert Joe Fattorini, broadcaster Samira Ahmed,sports presenter Steve Rider and writer Emma Kennedy take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an intrepid couple search for a home in Somerset with a budget of £900,000, complete with space for a helipad

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich look back at some the most therapeutic and poignant garden transformations, including a sensory safe space for two children with autism

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: With their car boot full of antiques, Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst head to an auction in Hertfordshire, hoping to make a profit on some old biscuit tins

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the silk and sequins at a major bhangra dance contest in Birmingham. It follows two rival teams as they shed blood, sweat and tears in their quest to win the coveted trophy

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore an ancient penny-farthing bicycle, a battered leather briefcase, an extraordinary portrait painted by mouth, and an old clocking-on clock

programme.title: Surviving the Virus: My Brother & Me
programme.desc: Documentary following doctors and twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken as they experience the professional and personal implications of the Covid-19 pandemic. In the third week of lockdown in the UK, Chris finds himself returning to work on the wards for the first time in ten years. Chris gets to work treating patients on the infectious disease wards, and he quickly learns just how unpredictable the virus can be. Meanwhile back in March 2020, Xand contracts Covid-19, which leaves him tired and sluggish, and he also develops an irregular heartbeat, which he suspects is part of the aftermath of his infection

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: An unlikely romance starts to blossom when 25-year-old Cat moves in and meets Liam. Meanwhile, Jake and Sanchez continue to clash after an alcohol-fuelled night

programme.title: Killed By My Debt
programme.desc: Factual drama telling the story of Jerome Rogers and the destructive power of debt. In January 2015, the south London 19-year-old finally got what he'd been working for - a new motorbike and his first real job as a courier. However, in the hands of bailiffs, two £65 traffic fines rose to over a thousand pounds. Some weeks, take-home pay in his zero-hours job was as low as £12. Under the pressure of his debt, Jerome went to some woods and committed suicide. With Chance Perdomo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the case of a woman executed for killing her husband in 1856, which inspired Thomas Hardy's Tess of the d'Urbervilles

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A driver whose dashcam recorded his heart attack and rescue by other motorists, and the rescuers who rushed to the aid of people swept out to sea, only to be dragged in themselves

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Catherine Southon help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about the ancient skill of enamelling

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Max's health causes concern, and a girls' talk reveals various home truths, some painful

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Actor and writer George Costigan, authors Clare Mackintosh and Wendy Holden, illustrator Nick Sharratt and poet Caleb Femi take on the general-knowledge champions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to Suffolk to help a couple find their dream country home with an annexe for one of their parents on a £525,000 budget, and also visits a pioneering farm

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock chooses some of her favourite Garden Rescue transformations, including a picture-perfect cottage garden complete with veg patches. The team also offers new tips

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst's trip takes in the Shires, where Tim shares some interesting sandwich recipes and uncovers an old coffin, while Catherine finds some medals

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: A report on the fake alcohol being served to British holiday makers in Greece. Plus, the children's toys that may contain gender altering chemicals

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of writer and director Kwame Kwei-Armah, who has spent the last two years serving as artistic director of London's Young Vic, overseeing successful productions including Death of a Salesman, Twelfth Night, Tree and the controversial Pulitzer Prize-winning Fairview. As the Young Vic celebrates its 50th anniversary, Alan hears how a young Southall boy called Ian Roberts became the artist Kwame Kwei-Armah. He came to prominence playing paramedic Finlay Newton in the BBC drama Casualty - and his groundbreaking play Elmina's Kitchen was one of the first by a black British writer staged at the National Theatre and in the West End

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees the retired detectives investigating possibly their most difficult cold case yet. A bloodstained bust is uncovered during construction work in a cemetery and the evidence points to the murder of an alternative-medicine practitioner seven years earlier. Further clues lead to the controversial world of cryopreservation - the practice of keeping clinically dead patients frozen to extend their life cycle. Sasha is determined they don't put a foot wrong - but with the sobering possibility that two perpetrators are still at large, the detectives are forced to go out on a limb

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers David and Harry Rich brothers reveal their favourite makeovers and also offer gardening tips and ideas. The first edition features a selection of family gardens, including a weird and wonderful Alice in Wonderland-inspired makeover, and one with a basketball court created for two children with mobility issues

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate a case of starvation and neglect from 1877 that saw a husband stand trial for the murder of his wife

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of the meerkats is poisoned, Mozart is allowed to return to the group, and love is in the air as some of the residents search for mates. Documentary following a family of meerkats living on the edge of the Kalahari desert, narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals how to pot tomatoes on to the next stage of their growth. He also pots up tender vegetables and plants containers for summer colour. Adam Frost enjoys the seasonal plants in his garden and shares plans to transform a part of his plot into a seating area using a few logs and plants. Arit Anderson fills a range of imaginative containers with a variety of plants, while Frances Tophill explores spring flowering trees and their benefits for bees. Plus, how one man has designed his home around his love of houseplants

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the first fixtures in four months, including St Helens v Catalans Dragons and Huddersfield Giants v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day five in Sheffield, where Shaun Murphy concludes his first-round match against Noppon Saengkham, and Mark Allen v Jamie Clarke begins

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day five at the Crucible Theatre, where Mark Selby concludes his first-round match against Jordan Browne, and Stephen Maguire v Martin Gould continues. Selby is a three-time champion of this event, with his last triumph coming in 2017 when he defeated John Higgins 18-15 in the final, but he was eliminated by Gary Wilson in the second round last year

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: A growing population puts pressure on the Whiskers group, and further stress is created when Big Cy from the rival Lazuli turns up. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Cornwall, the station's newest recruit, 18-year-old Shaunna, is following in the family tradition and her grandfather's footsteps. After a successful first rescue of a teenage holiday-maker, she hopes to become Newquay's first helmswoman. Meanwhile, Oban on the west coast of Scotland also has a new 18-year-old recruit, Andrew. However, he and the rest of the crew are given a painful reminder of the dangers all lifeboat volunteers face when they are woken in the middle of the night to rescue a fishing boat that is sinking

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Cuba's isolation has helped preserve many of its natural riches, creating unspoiled landscapes home to many enchanting animals. In the first of a two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson explores corners of the country that few outsiders have seen. Amongst the wonders he encounters is the bee hummingbird, the world's tiniest bird, and the spectacle of thousands of crabs migrating en masse. In this authored odyssey, the natural magic of one Earth's most intriguing countries is revealed

programme.title: Cuba: Castro vs the World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about Fidel Castro's leadership of Cuba, beginning with a look at his rise to power in 1959, which earned the hostility of the US and the support of the Soviet Union, a situation that culminated in the Cuban missile crisis. The programme also examines how Castro's government supported liberation struggles in other nations, including Algeria, Bolivia and Angola

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Danny Bhoy hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, warming up the audience before introducing routines by actor-comedian Miles Jupp and happy-go-lucky chancer Lee Nelson, aka Simon Brodkin

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Ireland. Action from the third and final match of the series, which took place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day five from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Mark Allen's first-round match against Jamie Clarke reached a conclusion

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the first-round matches on day five, where Shaun Murphy, Mark Allen and Mark Selby were among those in action

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades investigates the UK's espionage history through three items restored on the programme, including a bike and teapot linked to undercover operatives

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining four celebrities face two more challenges, beginning with the pairs task. Here they are split into teams and have to each serve up either John Torode's sushi selection or his veal schnitzel with spaetzle - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at Leyton Orient Football Club

programme.desc: Carole Baxter demonstrates the difference between a hardy annuals sown in a prepared bed as opposed to an unprepared one, and George Anderson shows off his Joppa allotment

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: Scientists and wildlife film-makers led by Steve Backshall, George McGavin and Gordon Buchanan explore Mount Bosavi, a giant extinct volcano covered by thick and largely unexplored rainforest in New Guinea. With the help of trackers from a remote tribe, they search for rare creatures and species to find evidence that may help stop the area's trees being felled for timber. The nest of the world's smallest parrot, an unusual beetle and new types of frog, gecko and bat are among the team's first discoveries

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day six at the Crucible Theatre, featuring the conclusion of Barry Hawkins v Alexander Ursenbacher and the start of John Higgins v Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: One of the meerkats returns from a liaison with a member of a rival family, and it soon becomes evident that she is pregnant. Meanwhile, Tosca has a short-lived change of fortune. Documentary providing insight into the daily struggles faced by a family of the animals living on the edge of the Kalahari desert. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the opening day of the First Test in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. The sides have been very evenly matched in this format over recent years, with the last two series between the nations ending in draws on English soil

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the chateaux of the Loire Valley to the heart of the Champagne region at Reims, beginning in the city of Orleans in north-central France. Here, he follows his Bradshaw's guide to the magnificent stained-glass windows of the Cathedral of Sainte-Croix, which tell the story of the heroine of France, Joan of Arc. Later, Michael takes a spin around the track at Le Mans in a French-built car, before making his way to the capital city of Paris. East of Paris, Michael finishes this leg of his journey in style with a tour of the cellars at Domaine Pommery in Reims

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in 1763 following a woman as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville star

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: When an old friend arrives in town, Margaret's hopes for the Soho move rest on whether she can tempt him to invest, while Justice Cunliffe makes Lydia an intriguing offer and Emily adjusts to the constraints of life in the Quigley household

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul visits the Isle of Ewe, where he meets Jane Grant, a local scallop farmer who skippers her own boat from the mainland

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day six from the Crucible, where Stephen Maguire v Martin Gould concluded round one, and Mark Williams v Stuart Bingham began in round two

programme.desc: Extended highlights of the first-round and second-round matches on day six from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Weekly magazine for the deaf and hard of hearing. With voiceover

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Over the past few months, more than 20,000 care home residents and staff have died of Covid-19 across the country. Panorama follows the struggle to protect residents, filming in two different homes over several months. What it reveals is the dedication of staff, the frustration of managers and heartache as more lives are lost. Alison Holt asks if care homes were abandoned to fight the virus alone

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades narrates an insight into the maritime history of three items restored on programme - a precious box, a book of cuttings about Captain Scott, and a handmade teddy bear

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four contenders battling for the last two places in the semi-finals, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champion Angellica Bell and finalists Les Dennis and Spencer Matthews. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders strive to remain in the reckoning

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace checks in with one of Britain's biggest biscuit factories, chatting to some of the workers via video calls to find out how they are coping during the Coronavirus pandemic. Gregg speaks to manager Nina Sparks, who he met during his visit back in 2017. Nina reassures him that all the ingredients for the biscuits are still getting through, including the flour that has been missing from many supermarket shelves due to a packaging shortage. Nina agrees to be Gregg's 'roving reporter', to show him what's changed on the production lines to meet demand, and what new safety measures have been put in place to keep the staff safe

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: The team pushes deeper into the rainforest on the tropical island of New Guinea, where cameraman Gordon Buchanan enlists the aid of local tribespeople to help him film birds of paradise performing their unusual courtship ritual. George McGavin has to contend with a giant crocodile, and Steve Backshall discovers a hidden network of caves never previously seen by humans

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day seven at the Crucible theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round, including John Higgins v Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day seven, featuring two second-round matches - Mark Williams v Stuart Bingham and Judd Trump v Yan Bingtao or Elliot Slessor

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Daisy unexpectedly gives birth in the wrong burrow, but Youssarian rescues two of her pups - which are later abandoned by the family babysitters and set out to find a new home on their own

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the second day of the First Test in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. The sides last met here in this format in 2016, when current captain Joe Root scored 254 in the first innings and added another 71 in the second as England recorded an emphatic 330-run victory

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The make-up artists are tasked to create a look for UK's best-selling gay magazine, working on their male models to deliver big, bold, high-fashion looks using materials that aren't strictly cosmetic. The MUA who produces the best look will be chosen for the editorial's lead image, but if the make-up isn't good enough it won't make the cut, and the magazine won't include it. The next day at Glow Up HQ Michelle Visage makes a surprise appearance on the judging panel, as the contestants are tasked with creating a look inspired by a club culture of their choice. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of roaming this week, with Sara Pascoe, Josh Widdicombe and Benjamin Zephaniah and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Rosie is excited as the Yateses depart for their annual summer holiday, but things are not exactly as she was expecting. In 2009, Si and Em Rosie and Ben down to Devon to meet Sophie's new baby Fliss - both of whom are presented as the embodiment of perfection by Nana. Em contemplates her less-than-perfect child by comparison, and has a realisation about how far her family has come in three years. Drama, starring David Tennant and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: Real-time sitcom following the hapless Stuart as he struggles through the worst half-hour of his life. Things haven't been going well for Stu and partner April since the birth of their baby. Faced with a make-or-break date, Stu has to contend with disruptions from his inappropriate dad, extortionist brother and an almighty argument erupting next door. Starring Lee Mack, Ellie White, Patrick Baladi, Neil Fitzmaurice and Clive Russell

programme.title: Squeamish About…
programme.desc: Spoof documentary in which reporter Michael Squeamish (Matt Berry) explores various subjects - using a unique selection of archive footage and playing fast-and-loose with the facts to create a joyously surreal whistle-stop tour of Britain. He begins by offering his unique take on British entertainment. From pigeon-racing to progressive jazz, beauty contests to the Buzzcocks, Squeamish takes viewers on an alternative journey through British culture, stopping at music hall, the scene and Joy Division along the way

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day seven from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two second-round matches, including John Higgins v Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the second-round matches on day seven from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Dear NHS Superstars
programme.desc: A selection of personal stories from some of Britain's best-loved stars paying tribute to the work of the NHS. Amanda Holden, Lenny Henry, Jo Brand and Alex Brooker are among the famous faces sharing their admiration for healthcare staff who have touched their lives in all sorts of ways. Others expressing their gratitude include Jack Whitehall, Adrian Lester, Shirley Ballas, Phil Wang, Rosie Jones, Kevin Bridges, Konnie Huq, Sue Perkins and Mel Giedroyc. Presented by writer, comedian and former doctor Adam Kay

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores how the invention of clockwork continues to influence modern life, using archive and three barn fixes - a centuries-old clock, a toy and a clocking-off machine

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and broadcaster Ashley John-Baptiste reveal the inspiring stories of people across the UK who lead spiritually and emotionally fulfilling lives. In this first edition, they set out to meet the people who have found inner peace, calm and equilibrium among the hustle and bustle of modern living. Kate takes to the open waters to experience how wild swimming has been a life-changer for some - and even a life-saver for others. Meanwhile, Ashley discovers how a little time living as a hermit could ease the pressures of busy lives

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day five from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Mark Allen's first-round match against Jamie Clarke reaches a conclusion and Barry Hawkins v Alexander Ursenbacher begins. Allen suffered a shock 10-7 defeat at the hands of China's Zhou Yuelong at this stage last year, but the Northern Irishman has been in consistent form this season and currently holds a career-high world ranking of fourth

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines the period between 1825 and 1918, in which four successive tsars faced a Russian society moving rapidly toward revolution. Lucy discovers how the 'Tsar-Liberator' Alexander II tried to free millions of serfs from slavery and examines the rise of radical writers and thinkers who sought to have a voice. She also uncovers the family life of the last tsar, Nicholas II, including the influence of Rasputin and questions whether the killing of the ruler and his family could have been avoided

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke charts the decline and fall of the Roman Empire through some of its hidden and most magical artistic treasures. On a trip to Leptis Magna in Libya, shortly after the overthrow of Colonel Gaddafi, he finds one of the best preserved Roman cities in the world and the cradle of later Roman art. Sooke discovers glorious mosaics that have never been filmed before, but also finds evidence of shocking neglect of Libya's Roman heritage under the Gaddafi regime

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: A tidal wave has carried Brizan's lighthouse into the forest and an arm of the sea has split the small town in two, a spectacle Lena foresaw in her dreams. The inhabitants panic, but Faust Ketchak sees it as a punishment against the devastating behaviour of people, while Lena and Ben uncover pollution on Brizan's construction site. In French

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The town's mayor calls a meeting to discuss plans to deal with the cloud, which might have a devastating effect on the surfers. In French

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Riviera's artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote d'Azur's beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke charts the decline and fall of the Roman Empire through some of its hidden and most magical artistic treasures. On a trip to Leptis Magna in Libya, shortly after the overthrow of Colonel Gaddafi, he finds one of the best preserved Roman cities in the world and the cradle of later Roman art. Sooke discovers glorious mosaics that have never been filmed before, but also finds evidence of shocking neglect of Libya's Roman heritage under the Gaddafi regime

programme.title: Empire of the Tsars: Romanov Russia with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines the period between 1825 and 1918, in which four successive tsars faced a Russian society moving rapidly toward revolution. Lucy discovers how the 'Tsar-Liberator' Alexander II tried to free millions of serfs from slavery and examines the rise of radical writers and thinkers who sought to have a voice. She also uncovers the family life of the last tsar, Nicholas II, including the influence of Rasputin and questions whether the killing of the ruler and his family could have been avoided

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session on day six from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where Stephen Maguire v Martin Gould brings round one to a close and the second-round contest between Mark Williams and Stuart Bingham gets under way. Maguire reached the quarter-finals last year, where he lost to eventual champion Judd Trump, and will be looking to build on his triumph in June's Tour Championship

programme.title: Britain's Nuclear Secrets: Inside Sellafield
programme.desc: Physicist Jim Al-Khalili explores the history of the nuclear facility near the village of Seascale in Cumbria, investigating Britain's attempts - past, present and future - to harness the power of the atom. He examines the ways waste and spent fuel rods have been stored there over the past 70 years and looks at the latest attempts to try to clean some of it up, from storage in vast open-air ponds to encasing pieces of old reactors in concrete blocks

programme.title: Hiroshima
programme.desc: Docu-drama charting the political and social impact of the nuclear attack on the Japanese city of Hiroshima in 1945. Based on facts drawn from survivors' testimony, official documents and interviews, the film recreates the horror of the explosion and builds a detailed account of the reasons behind the decision to drop the bomb. Narrated by John Hurt

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary examining how nuclear power has changed the world since the bombing of Hiroshima. The film is comprised entirely of archive footage, and explores the positive and negative aspects of the atomic age, contrasting protest marches, the Cold War and the Chernobyl and Fukishima disasters with advances in medical science, such as X rays and MRI scanners. With music by Mogwai

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: First in a five-part series in which Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the close relationship between discoveries in anatomy and the works of art that illustrate them. He begins by focusing on Roman anatomist Claudius Galen and artist and part-time dissector Leonardo da Vinci

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford examines the story of Andreas Vesalius, who in 1537 became the most famous anatomist in Europe. He went on to produce the first complete account of the human body and how to dissect it, and his drawings set the standard for anatomical art for centuries to come, earning him the title of 'the founder of modern anatomy'

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: Clemency Burton-Hill explores the life of Antonio Stradivari, a 17th-century violin-maker who created over one thousand stringed instruments. Visiting the Stradivarius exhibition at the Ashmolean Museum in Oxford, she examines four of the violins and she is joined by 2002 Young Musician of the Year winner Jennifer Pike to test some of them out

programme.title: Britain's Nuclear Secrets: Inside Sellafield
programme.desc: Physicist Jim Al-Khalili explores the history of the nuclear facility near the village of Seascale in Cumbria, investigating Britain's attempts - past, present and future - to harness the power of the atom. He examines the ways waste and spent fuel rods have been stored there over the past 70 years and looks at the latest attempts to try to clean some of it up, from storage in vast open-air ponds to encasing pieces of old reactors in concrete blocks

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day seven from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two second-round matches, including John Higgins v Kurt Maflin to a conclusion. Australian maestro Neil Robertson was the first player to book his place in the quarter-finals last year, comfortably defeating 2005 champion Shaun Murphy of England 13-6

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The outing to Box Hill ends in disaster when Emma insults Miss Bates. After being berated by Knightley, she realises her behaviour was shameful and tries to make amends, but Jane refuses to speak to her. When Frank's overbearing aunt dies, the Westons expect him to propose to Emma, but his subsequent actions start a chain of events that shocks the heroine, resulting in the realisation of a truth she previously dismissed. Romola Garai and Jonny Lee Miller star in the final instalment of Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel

programme.title: What We Did on Our Holiday (2014)
programme.desc: A couple whose marriage has hit a rocky patch take a trip to Scotland with their three children, to visit the husband's father on his 75th birthday. They try to conceal their marital strife to avoid spoiling the occasion, but have to contend with the kids giving away all their secrets. Comedy drama, starring Rosamund Pike, David Tennant and Billy Connolly

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Portrait of a Lifetime
programme.desc: A programme celebrating Billy Connolly's 75th birthday and 50 years in the business, as three Scottish artists, John Byrne, Jack Vettriano and Rachel Maclean, each create a new portrait of the comedian and actor. As he sits with each artist, Billy talks about his remarkable career which has taken him from musician and stand-up to Hollywood star and national treasure

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how royal collecting has changed since the days of Queen Victoria. He also reveals how women, both queens and consorts, made their mark on the Collection, and used art to steady the ship of monarchy through an uncertain age

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: Jewellery designer Shaun Leane goes behind the scenes at an exhibition of Elizabethan and Jacobean gems at the Museum of London. The precious stones were discovered during the demolition of the city's Cheapside district at the beginning of the 20th century, and the presenter uncovers a story of forgery and murder as he sets out to find why they were buried, who they belonged to and why they remained in their hiding place for years

programme.title: Secret Knowledge
programme.desc: To mark 250 years since William Hogarth's death, ceramics expert Lars Tharp tries to recover a precious terracotta sculpture of the artist's pet pug Trump. The beloved animal features prominently in the painter's self-portrait, and as Lars explains, Hogarth's obsession with this most colourful of breeds played a pivotal role in his life. This canine odyssey not only examines his iconic artworks, but delves into a world of satire, salaciousness and secrets

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Thailand, from the northern city of Chiang Mai to Kanchanaburi on the River Kwai and from Bangkok to the southern beach resort of Hua Hin. Along the way, he tries his hand at the art of umbrella-making and learns about northern Thai cookery. He also stops off to help at a hospital for elephants wounded by landmines and admires the ruined temples at the former Siamese capital, Ayutthaya

Expert rock movers Bevel, Zip and Pop arrive, but can't move it either. Looking for things to help them, Bitz finds some logs to roll the rock, but Bevel stops to eat a banana and the rock rolls off out of control, causing the logs to go everywhere. They then consider floating the rock

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Kwazii and Dashi become tangled up in a deep-sea creature, but when a geyser blasts it toward the surface of the water, the gang must try to stop it

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster struggles to find the perfect epic birthday present fo Bad Idea Puppy.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Drummer D has his drumsticks but nothing to play on, so his friends help him find something he can use to deliver a dazzling solo

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin has lost his favourite socks and is convinced that someone in Justin's House must have taken them, he even starts to suspect his trusty friends!

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: An over enthusiastic Raa Raa breaks Pia's new Jungle Gym, but he manages to fix it so everyone can have a turn

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The young sheep wants to learn to fly, so he asks Otus for lessons

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Charlie upsets Bing when he doesn't want to play the games he planned, but the pair soon find that making a noise in the kitchen is lots of fun

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The friends learn about what upside down means when they watch a gallery owner hang some paintings. Back on the boat, they help put the Very Important Lady the right way up

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: On a cold day in Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies find big snowballs, dance with a snowman and watch children make a caterpillar from snow

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with oranges and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: When Meepa becomes class monitor she turns super bossy, making her monster friends feel fed up

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid is enlisted to make a wedding cake for an unhappy bride, and makes his way to a bakery to find an assistant

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the docks to get on board an old sailing ship.

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: There is an unusual smell in the house that Pablo finds very distracting. In the Art World the Aroma becomes a swirling character that makes it hard to think or speak at all

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Rhino has a problem - she is so sad. The Patchwork Pals set about lightening her mood

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Finlay and his family travel on a ferry to a nearby island. it is not long before Finlay finds an old canon and plays at fighting off imaginary pirate ships

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: A fable from a collection of African folk tales about why a parrot cannot keep a secret

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesn't like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbs's garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools. Maybe that will encourage him back?

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The sheep creates an imaginary friend when Yabba will not play with him

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack buys Daisy a puppy called Little Mo. Moira says no pets are allowed in the Big Hub, so Molly and Mack try to hide Little Mo, but then she escapes

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah finds an umbrella that does not seem to like the rain

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: When Mo wakes up with a cold, who s there to take over mum duties? Tee of course!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Letter A discovers a magic red nose and gets up to all sorts of five-letter mischief, introducing the words prank, stink, trunk, stomp, stamp, plank and stunt

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee has lots of fun with peas - seeing peas on a plate, peas in a pod - and even a pea in flight. She also encourages a friend to say 'peas'

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Four is the new block on the block and he can't wait to share how much he loves to be square! Learn all about the number 4 with Numberblock Four

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop and Bing visit the park where they see Pando on a skateboard and decide to try it out for themselves - but discover that it is much harder to balance than it looks

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: Great BigHoo, Toodloo, Chickedy and Chick learn all about getting wet when they have an encounter with a garden hose

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin Fletcher and his friends go on an adventure to a dinosaur park. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble visits Shopkeeper Tumble in The Shop with the Lot to buy Tumble Ted a birthday present

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po enjoy a game of musical statues

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Lemon is annoyed the Leafs think her antlers are a tree, and won't leave her alone. The Olobobs make Treewheeler, who takes the Leafs on an exciting rocket ride in the sky

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: One day Pablo hears a strange sound and discovers his mother has some squeaky new shoes. He wants to know what they're trying to say. Are the shoes crying or laughing?

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo and his friends question change in the world around them, from eggs that become birds to a child becoming a grown up

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we meet a new family, five-year-old twins Daisy and Lily. They live near the seaside with their mum, dad and younger twin sisters Molly and Nell. The family has a busy breakfast time with all those mouths to feed, and Daisy and Lily are in charge of counting the eggs. Later, Mum takes the girls out for some fun on the beach where they make a picture using pebbles and seaweed. Back at home Daisy and Lily get to work in their toy kitchen, making pretend soup for all the family. But will their sausage roll and vegetable soup be a success at their super-soup party?

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Catie drives a supercar, shows us around a really fancy superyacht and then challenges herself to drive a stretch limousine around her track without spilling any drinks

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook creates the most delicious soup anyone has ever tasted, and Captain Captain orders him to make more so they can sell it and become rich

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter volunteers to take Mrs Tiggy-Winkle home in time for her winter hibernation, little does he know how much trouble a sleepy hedgehog can be!

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns what happens to the food we eat and discovers how meringue is made

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz has created a super strawberry, dreamy creamy, nicely icely, bake-a-licious cake that is so tasty Bob suggests she makes more so she can sell them to the whole world

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch worries his Halloween pumpkin has not grown big enough, and decides to raid the Gua Rusa cave in Borneo for some super-powered guano fertiliser

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Captain Adorable recruits Gail's toy mascot to be his new sidekick

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie forgets her reading book, so Waffle takes it to her PE class at school

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran have their own sports day, including an egg and spoon race.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The duo visit a large department store looking for some new toys

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A Skymoo accidentally knocks the Iron Chicken out of her nest, and falling to the surface of the Clangers' planet she hurts her wing. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The friends run around the garden so much that Makka Pakka cannot wash their faces

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Pearl Mackie reads Interstellar Cinderella, by Deborah Underwood and Meg Hunt

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the murder of a military veteran by his estranged wife in 1825

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Norwich, Brynmawr in south Wales and Maidstone in Kent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from the Lincolnshire Antiques and Home show, where experts Gary Pe and Jonathan Pratt help two teams to bag a bargain or two to take to auction. Plus, Eric uncovers the intriguing story behind a collection of postcards of the First World War era

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia's time off is interrupted alarmingly, and Laila's schooling causes conflict

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Sophie Faldo, David Domoney, Frances Tophill, James Tanner and Will Kirk form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a retired couple search for a new home in rural Somerset with a budget of £700,000, and firm ideas on the vision for their perfect country home. Along the way, Jonnie masters the thrilling sport of land yachting at Brean Sands, where thanks to a prevailing wind he picks up the pace along this picturesque stretch of beach

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: The team recalls some of their favourite quirky garden rescues, including a music festival-inspired plot featuring an air-raid shelter, and a fusion of Yorkshire and Japan

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst explore the New Forest and Southampton Water, and learn about tide milling. Items under the hammer include a mantle clock and a pocket watch

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The 2007 Christmas episode, in which the truth is out about Max and Stacey. It must have seemed like the perfect way to end a lovely Christmas dinner - the Branning and Slater clans settling down together to watch Bradley and Stacey's wedding DVD. Unfortunately, the families weren't expecting to see the blushing bride kissing her new father-in-law as he begged her to resume their illicit affair. Now, everyone has to digest this alarming revelation along with their turkey - and no doubt it's going to be wronged spouses Tanya and Bradley who will be hit the hardest

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric's operation takes place on the same day as Guy's disciplinary hearing, forcing him to choose between treatment that could his life and his need to see Guy face the consequences of his actions. Sacha's silent treatment leaves Essie convinced he knows something about Frankie's disappearance, and Chloe tries her hand at Internet dating - only for one of her matches to be admitted as a patient

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Gates is desperate to sever his ties with Jackie after being duped into covering up her crime, but he is plunged into an even more dangerous situation as Arnott and Fleming close in on his secret and is forced to choose between his marriage and his career. Crime thriller about a detective sergeant working in anti-corruption who is assigned to investigate a respected senior officer. Starring Lennie James, Martin Compston, Vicky McClure, Neil Morrissey and Gina McKee

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of reconnecting with nature. She tries giving up environmentally destructive habits like driving and using plastic packaging and going camping in the Welsh Hills, where she meets a family who have embraced the delights of living off-grid

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Alan Cumming and Nick Grimshaw are among the special guests joining Michelle for a special Pride episode. Chef Jaime Laurita drops in to bake a special pride cake, and Michelle performs a long-distance marriage ceremony for a couple in Scotland

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the murder of a military veteran by his estranged wife in 1825

programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from Wadebridge in Cornwall as experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help the teams find antiques to sell on at auction.
