programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Poker pro Liv Boeree explains how winning or losing millions can come down to keeping one's nerve in the most fleeting of moments and Moshi Monsters creator Michael Acton Smith, who has sold more than 200 million trading cards, reveals why he is not afraid to risk failure to discover the next big thing. Ruth also talks to angel investor Lauren Maillian and Eamonn meets city stockbroker Andrew Craig to learn about the arcane workings of the world of high finance

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer heads to Alaska, America's largest state and home to some of the most breathtaking scenery on the planet. She takes a helicopter trip up to an ice field for a dog-sledding adventure, and later witnesses the majesty of the Margery glacier, a 21-mile stretch of ice in the Glacier Bay National Park. On board Holland America's ship Nieuw Amsterdam, Jane goes backstage to meet the cast of the nightly musicals, has a cooking lesson from the executive chef and reveals how to make the most of a lazy day in port

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: House Busters
programme.desc: Janet Ellis meets a homeowner who feels trapped in her property, and has seen various attempts to sell it fall through. She receives advice from a feng shui expert, who claims she is too fixated on the past, a 'home healer', who diagnoses low energy levels in the property, and a mystic who announces that the tree outside her bedroom window has to go

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice offers advice to would-be sellers in Cotham, Bristol, whose four-bedroom bungalow has been on the market for four months. She suggests revamping the shabby sitting room to enhance its ambience and updating the mismatched kitchen with a lick of paint, tile jewellery and new flooring, before turning her attention to sprucing up the two bathrooms

programme.title: Run Wild, Run Free (1969)
programme.desc: Withdrawn, mute 10-year-old Philip is ignored by his parents and goes off to explore Dartmoor alone, where he meets a retired colonel who shares his knowledge of Devon's wildlife. Under the older man's nurturing tutelage, the boy begins to tame a wild white colt, but fate proffers a series of setbacks just as real progress is being made. Children's drama, starring John Mills, Mark Lester, Sylvia Syms and Bernard Miles

programme.title: Rugrats in Paris: The Movie (2000)
programme.desc: The lucky little tots embark on a trip to a French theme park - but while the rest of the gang are intent on enjoying themselves, Chuckie is distracted by his wish for a new mum. He sets his sights on the owner of the amusement park as a prospective partner for his single dad. Animated adventure, with the voices of Christine Cavanaugh, Elizabeth Daily, Cheryl Chase, Kath Soucie and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda 2 (2011)
programme.desc: Martial arts master panda Po continues to hone his skills when an evil peacock, who was banished from China for trying to build a deadly new weapon, returns to cause more chaos. It falls to Po to defeat him, but he suffers strange flashbacks revealing a connection between his past and the sinister bird. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jack Black and Gary Oldman

programme.title: The Pink Panther Strikes Again (1976)
programme.desc: Inspector Clouseau fends off a murder syndicate led by his former police chief Dreyfus, who has been driven insane by his hatred for the bungling detective. However, doing away with the lucky lawman proves harder than anticipated - even for the world's best assassins. Comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Herbert Lom, Lesley-Anne Down, Burt Kwouk and Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Only the Brave (2017)
programme.desc: Eric Marsh, the superintendent of a group of elite firefighters, is pushed to his limit when he and his crew tackle a historic wildfire. Fact-based drama, starring Josh Brolin, Jennifer Connelly and Andie MacDowell. Directed by Joseph Kosinski, helmer of Tron: Legacy and Top Gun: Maverick

programme.title: Roman J. Israel, Esq. (2018)
programme.desc: A driven, idealistic defence attorney gets mixed up in a series of events that lead to a crisis and the necessity for extreme action. Crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Colin Farrell and Carmen Ejogo

programme.title: 2 Guns (2013)
programme.desc: Two undercover agents - one working for the DEA the other for the Navy - infiltrate a drug cartel, only for the planned sting operation to go disastrously wrong. Both operatives become fugitives and must work together to survive - but both keep their true identity a secret, mistakenly thinking the other is a real criminal. Action adventure, starring Denzel Washington and Mark Wahlberg

programme.title: For Those in Peril (2013)
programme.desc: The sinking of a fishing boat from a Scottish coastal village leaves only one survivor. The superstitious locals blame him for the deaths of the other men and ostracise him, but he refuses to believe that the rest of the crew, including his own brother, are dead and tries to find a way to save them. Drama, starring George MacKay, Kate Dickie and Michael Smiley

programme.title: Garfield 2: A Tale of Two Kitties (2006)
programme.desc: The lazy moggy stows away in his owner's luggage and ends up in London. He is promptly mistaken for an English upper-class cat, who has recently inherited a grand estate, and moves into his stately home - much to the dismay of the real aristocratic feline's conniving relation, who is bent on swindling him out of his money. Family comedy, starring Breckin Meyer, Billy Connolly and Jennifer Love Hewitt, with the voices of Bill Murray and Tim Curry

programme.title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid: The Long Haul (2017)
programme.desc: Youngster Greg Heffley hatches a plot to re-route a family road trip towards a videogame convention. Comedy sequel, starring Jason Drucker, Alicia Silverstone and Tom Everett Scott

programme.title: Parental Guidance (2012)
programme.desc: A busy mother turns to her parents for help in looking after her three children while she and her husband are working. However, family relations are soon strained to breaking point when it becomes clear that the two generations have very different ideas about childcare. Comedy, with Billy Crystal, Bette Midler and Marisa Tomei

programme.title: Romancing the Stone (1984)
programme.desc: A romantic novelist goes in search of her kidnapped sister in the steamy jungles of South America, only to become the target of a villainous general who demands as a ransom the valuable treasure map in her possession. Help is at hand in the form of a craggy daredevil fortune hunter, who has his eye on a share of the profits. Comedy adventure, starring Michael Douglas, Kathleen Turner and Danny DeVito

programme.title: Gods of Egypt (2016)
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian god is driven into exile when his evil brother murders their father and seizes his throne. He enlists the aid of a human thief in restoring justice, and in return offers to help the mortal to bring his murdered lover back to life. Fantasy adventure, starring Nikolaj Coster-Waldau, Brenton Thwaites, Gerard Butler and Chadwick Boseman

programme.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit (2014)
programme.desc: A former soldier injured in the line of duty takes up a new career as a financial analyst for the CIA. However, his deskbound position changes to a dangerous undercover assignment when he discovers a plot to destroy the US economy. Spy thriller inspired by Tom Clancy's series of novels, starring Chris Pine, Keira Knightley, Kevin Costner and Kenneth Branagh, who also directed

programme.title: The Dressmaker (2015)
programme.desc: In 1950s Australia, a fashion designer returns to her remote home town after forging a successful career in Paris. She reconciles with her mother, falls in love and transforms the lives of the local women - getting even with her enemies in the process. Drama based on the novel by Rosalie Ham, starring Kate Winslet, Judy Davis, Hugo Weaving and Liam Hemsworth

programme.title: A Touch of Sin (2013)
programme.desc: Anthology of four stories of ordinary people living in China, who are each driven to violent ends. A miner takes the law into his own hands to fight corruption, a migrant adjusts to owning a gun, a sauna employee hits back against an abusive client, and a factory worker is pushed to the limit by a series of degrading tasks. Drama, starring Zhao Tao, Jiang Wu, Wang Baoqiang and Luo Lanshan. In Mandarin, Cantonese and English

programme.title: The Book of Life (2014)
programme.desc: A man who longs to be a musician instead of a bullfighter like his father has to compete with a childhood friend when they fall for the same woman. Their love triangle catches the eye of the spirits of the dead, leading him on a journey through their bizarre world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Diego Luna, Zoe Saldana and Channing Tatum

programme.title: Crack in the World (1965)
programme.desc: A dying scientist and his colleagues conduct experiments to harness the Earth's inner energy that seem set to end in disaster. Sci-fi thriller, with Dana Andrews, Janette Scott, Kieron Moore and Alexander Knox

programme.title: Ice Age: The Meltdown (2006)
programme.desc: Sid the sloth, Manny the mammoth and Diego the sabre-toothed tiger grimly realise the good life is about to come to an abrupt end as flood waters threaten their homes. However, a vulture suggests they could turn an old giant tree into a boat and make their escape, so the three friends set off in search of it. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Ray Romano, John Leguizamo and Denis Leary

programme.title: Megamind (2010)
programme.desc: An evil genius finally succeeds in killing the superhero who has foiled his every evil scheme, but finds life without an enemy boring. To fill the gap, he transforms an ordinary man into a worthy adversary - but when his creation turns evil, it falls to the villain to save the world. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Ferrell, Tina Fey and Brad Pitt

programme.title: The Core (2003)
programme.desc: A chain of bizarre and deadly incidents in several countries leads scientists to the conclusion that the Earth's core has stopped spinning. As chaos erupts around the world, experts hatch a plan to halt the destruction and save the human race - but it involves venturing deep below the planet's surface to detonate a nuclear bomb. Sci-fi thriller, starring Aaron Eckhart, Hilary Swank, Stanley Tucci and DJ Qualls

programme.title: Star Trek Beyond (2016)
programme.desc: The USS Enterprise sets out on a rescue mission which turns out to be a deadly trap set by a mysterious warlord. The crew are left stranded on an unexplored planet, and join forces with a fugitive alien scavenger to find a way off the hostile world and prevent a terrible weapon being unleashed upon the galaxy. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pine, Zachary Quinto and Idris Elba

programme.title: Me and Earl and the Dying Girl (2015)
programme.desc: An awkward teenager shuns all kinds of social interaction, apart from making films with his best friend. His well-meaning mother urges him to spend time with a girl who has been diagnosed with leukaemia, and they unexpectedly develop a firm friendship. However, his life is changed forever as her condition gets worse. Comedy drama based on Jesse Andrews' novel, starring Thomas Mann, RJ Cyler and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: The Daughter (2015)
programme.desc: A man returning home for his father's wedding uncovers a secret that threatens to destroy the lives of those he left behind. Drama, with Paul Schneider and Geoffrey Rush

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly is determined to make friends with her reserved and softly spoken neighbours, the Kremps, so she bombards them with invitations to a barbecue. Meanwhile, Barry and Erica battle for control of the family's telephone line and beg their parents to get a second one installed. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly reignites a long-standing feud after she invites Murray's unreliable brother to visit at Thanksgiving. Meanwhile, Adam upsets the balance of power between him and Barry after he wins a ball game between them for the first time. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Contestants from in and around Glasgow compete for the cash prize of £1,000, with Mandi and Ally, Stephen and Lucy, and Judith and Charlene hoping to impress with their menus

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Tom plans a wedding at his favourite theme park, but his down-to-earth fiancee Becca has always dreamed of being treated like a princess on her special day

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The four couples who decided to renew their vows return home and begin the real challenge of integrating their lives and making their new relationships work

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel and Joe volunteer at the school dance, but get themselves kicked out by the school's vice principal for bad behaviour. Meanwhile, Ryder considers breaking up with Stella. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel takes Joe's side when he is involved in a hit-and-run accident, until she discovers that the other driver is her beloved hairdresser Calista. Meanwhile, Ryder tries to win Stella back during lessons, much to the frustration of their teacher. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The squad members worry about Diaz when she responds to an active shooter alert, but are instructed to stay back at the precinct building and are powerless to help. Peralta and Boyle try to find ways to assist Diaz, Jeffords has an existential crisis, while Santiago and Gina attempt to repair the broken toilet in the ladies' room

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Diaz and Santiago team up to take down a ruthless killer who has managed to elude them for seven years. Jeffords helps Peralta to tackle the chores on his wedding planning list. Captain Holt and his rival Olivia Crawford try to convince each other to step down from the campaign to become Police Commissioner

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The prodigy dismantles the refrigerator to stop it making an annoying humming noise and is then forced to get a paper round to pay for the repairs to the damaged appliance. Comedy spin-off prequel to The Big Bang Theory, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj accepts Sheldon's offer to work for him as he is desperate to avoid being sent back to India. Howard starts spending more time with Leonard and Penny, who would rather be left alone. Johnny Galecki, Simon Helberg and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.desc: Joe is forced to quickly make a decision that could affect his and Mel's marriage after his new wife drops a bombshell at the ceremony. Meanwhile, Lennox is caught between Zander and Marco, and Ryder struggles to find the right time to make an announcement about college

programme.title: Melissa & Joey

Ryder takes an online course, and Zander tries to win back Lennox - but Marco has already decided to make a commitment to her. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly replaces Murray's favourite chair with a new one without consulting him first, causing him to go on the warpath. Adam becomes tired of being picked on during the bus ride to school, but soon comes to regret enlisting Barry to help fend off the bully

programme.desc: Murray intervenes when Adam asks Beverly to help him avoid the humiliation of taking a national physical fitness test at school. Meanwhile, Erica decides to have some fun at Barry's expense when he tries to woo her French pen pal

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago investigate a difficult 'he said, she said' case. Captain Holt becomes suspicious that a cunning escape from jail has taken place after discovering that his lifelong criminal arch-nemesis has died in a prison transport accident

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, starring Joe Thomas and Hannah Tointon

For some it's a chance to re-live the highs and update us on their relationships, but for those who parted on a sour note, it's the final opportunity to confront their exes and gain closure

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The boys want to become more sporty, so Barry Kripke leads them in a fencing lesson, while Penny, Amy and Bernadette help Stuart, who wants to attract more female customers to the comic-book store

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy's attempts to celebrate her birthday with an intimate night at home are interrupted by the news that Bernadette has gone into labour. However, it transpires that Howard, Raj and Stuart may have rushed the expectant mother to the hospital too early, with Raj having difficulty keeping the gender of the unborn child a secret

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After spending the holidays apart, the gang get together to hear about Sheldon's and Amy's trip to his mother's, Leonard and Penny recall their Christmas tree adventure, and Howard and Bernadette open up about their first days as parents

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Simon passes his driving test, so the lads decide to take a trip to a theme park. However, things start to go wrong when their route is obstructed by a funeral cortege and the newly qualified motorist has an accident in the car park. Later, Will regrets ever making the journey when he disgraces himself on a rollercoaster

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will discovers that attending a mixed-sex school is not so bad after all when he manages to get a date with the most popular girl in the year above. programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Who'd a Thought It in Grafty Green, Kent, where partners Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin hope to impress their fellow guests. On arrival, Liz and Brian are excited over their two hot tubs, and Jackie and Paul are more than happy with the complimentary champagne. However, Matthew and Liam are underwhelmed by the cleanliness levels

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the New Osborne Hotel in Blackpool on their second night, where proprietor Matthew Bracegirdle and entertainment manager Liam hope their guests feel at home in their family friendly establishment. However, Jackie and Paul are unimpressed by their small room, and Joe and Alex find the cleanliness levels lacking

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Fairy Falls Hotel in Trefriw, Conwy County Borough, where B&B newcomers Jackie and Paul Langshaw hope to impress the other contestants. They take their guests to a local maze in the afternoon and, after an uncomfortable night's sleep for Alex, some of the breakfast orders are misplaced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Husband and wife Liz and Brian Jardine welcome their guests to The Great Trewern B&B, Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where they enjoy the quiet life. Joe and Alex feel far away from it all, but the shared bathroom facilities fail to impress the other guests. At dinner, talk turns to first impressions and it appears it is all water under the bridge for Jackie and Joe, but she remains upset with the feedback Matthew and Liam gave her

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one final time to find out what they have been paid. Joe Mallett and Alex Rea Baldwin kick things off by confronting Matthew Bracegirdle and Liam Halewood about their comments regarding the cleanliness of their establishment, and Jackie and Paul Langshaw complain about their poor breakfast score

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pedro the giant anteater is about to be reunited with long-term mate Bliss, but falls ill and faces a major operation, with an uncertain outcome. Chester Zoo's extremely rare bald ibises produce a batch of new chicks, which are destined to be released back into the wild in southern Spain. There is also a chance to witness the reunion of rhino couple Beni and Asha, who have been kept separate for four years

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A one-year-old St Bernard suffering with hip dysplasia is brought to the clinic by the Warden family. Having recently lost the the dog's brother to a similar illness, emotions are running high. Meanwhile, Noel treats a 13-year-old collie that has been hit by a car, and tries to devise a new bionic paw for a cat with a badly deformed hind foot

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim battles to get a fence built for their marauding flock of 25 sheep, and Krys battles to balance the books. They race to get their chateau ready for their first paying guests as Lee struggles with a chandelier. Elaine and David continue the search for their dream property, visiting a 17th-century ex-priory in Normandy and taking on board all that Dick has taught them

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Following the work of Ed and Rowena Waghorn as they continue to build a handcrafted, five-bedroom house on an eight acre smallholding in Herefordshire, 10 years in the making. Progress on the build was slow as Ed decided to do most of the work himself, and after four years the house was still a shell. In 2012 Kevin McCloud decided to follow the build for another five years. In the end, it's just possible this house might have turned out to be a masterpiece

programme.title: Japan's Tsunami: How It Happened
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the scientific factors behind the magnitude nine earthquake and the resultant tsunami that struck Japan on March 11, 2011. Professor of geological sciences Roger Bilham views the effects of the devastation from the air, and journalist Callum Macrae travels to the north of the country, where survivors in the fishing villages of Sendai and Ofunato are struggling to cope in the aftermath

programme.desc: The air ambulance teams are called out to help a man who is having a heart attack, and there is a dramatic night-time car crash in Norfolk

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to Monmouthshire, where Peter and Amanda Copp want to rope their guests into the world of 'glamping' at Hidden Valley Yurts. John has a hairy encounter in his yurt, and the shared bathroom facilities fail to impress David and Nikki. The next morning, the DIY breakfast proves underwhelming for some, while the hosts discover that the great outdoors is not everyone's cup of tea

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former hotel inspector and owner David Stanbridge and manager Nikki Deans welcome the contestants to the Cley Hall Hotel in Spalding, Lincolnshire. Peter and Amanda are impressed by their room, but Simon and Lyn are unhappy with the state of the carpet. The next morning, the guests decide to put their eight-minute breakfast rule to the test, and some of them have concerns about Nikki's waitressing skills

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and confront their critics. programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: At Chester Zoo, Rothschild giraffe Tula is a week overdue after a 13-month pregnancy. But keepers are anxious that history could repeat itself - she gave birth to a stillborn calf last year. Cameras also follow this year's brood of penguin chicks. They are about to face the ordeal of joining the main pool, where they must compete for fish with the adult penguins. And baby silvery gibbon Eko learns how to swing between ropes - 50 feet up

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Stephen Yeoman and Anita Findlay, who want to build a cutting-edge, post-industrial house covered in rusty metal. However, their prominent riverside plot in the traditional and architecturally conservative area of South Downs means everyone will be watching. The project proves to be a real rollercoaster when the fitting of the rusty steel cladding goes wrong, cash flow problems threaten to bring work to a halt, and the couple announce that a baby is on the way

However, the next morning is not so harmonious as the guests jostle for access to the only available shower

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Bournemouth, where owners John and Chris Griffith welcome their guests to the Bed & Breakfast by the Beach. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Lansbury in Whitby, North Yorkshire. All the guests are keen to have a good look around their rooms after falling victim to owner Jill Winspear's criticism earlier in the week - but her attempts at a guided tour backfire, and the breakfast service fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group's final destination is Charlie and Angie Hobson's Castle House in Denbigh, north Wales. Some of the guests are unimpressed by a day trip to a local spa - but back at the B&B, the previously critical Jill is full of praise

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners discover what the other competitors thought of their businesses by opening the cash-filled envelopes, having put in what they believed their hosts should receive. They meet for one last time to learn who has been declared the best value B&B of the week

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's 18 chimpanzees face upheaval when new female chimp Vila arrives from a zoo in Germany. Meanwhile, nine-year-old cheetah mum Kinky Tail is vomiting after meals, and her three-year-old daughter Safi must watch and wait as her mum is taken to the zoo's medical centre. Cameras also follow Asian short-clawed otter Annie, who recently gave birth to a large litter of five pups

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman and her sons bring in their springer spaniel puppy for complicated surgery on its badly deformed hind leg. The owner of an eight-year-old cocker spaniel waits to hear if her dog's cancer has spread, and the vet treats a two-year-old cat, which is dragging one of its back legs

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Angel invite Tim and Krys over to their place as Dick shows Tim how to put on a gourmet weekend, and Angel has a heart-to-heart chat about chateau-living with Krys. Lee wrestles with a curtain in the shower room on arrival day for his and Belinda's first paying guests. Plus, Angela and Steve invite eight friends over to test-run their tandem holiday business

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Andy and Nicky Bruce, who were building an experimental amphibious house on a small island on the Thames in Buckinghamshire. They spent £1.2million on one of the most ambitious Grand Designs ever, but the logistical headache of building on an island only accessible by a narrow footbridge soon became clear. Heavy machinery was swept away by the river, contractors were changed, and the site was hit by severe storms, causing delays and resulting in an unfinished and untested building. Kevin returns to find out what living on a regularly flooded piece of land is like, and whether the floating home performed when the river burst its banks in January 2015

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: A look at the increasing number of crimes committed by women, with PC Tom Gregory investigating an early evening report of a group of women attacking a lone girl. PC Jon Simpson visits the male victim of an opportunist robbery by a woman at his home, and a 12-year-old girl who has endured years of bullying gives a statement to police about the female teen gang who set fire to her hair

programme.desc: Offbeat tourism show in which Richard Ayoade is joined by celebrity guests to enjoy the most efficient weekend break possible. He begins in Barcelona with comedienne and actress Kathy Burke, experiencing the best of this popular holiday destination in just 48 hours. The pair sample adventurous cuisine, including potatoes coated in edible clay, and a cava tasting session quickly descends into disarray. Other highlights include a visit to the Barcelona Football Club museum, a guided tricycle tour, and a trip to see the surrealist work of Catalan artist Joan Miro

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by fellow comedian Adam Hills on a whirlwind weekend in Turkey's largest city. He wants to show The Last Leg host how to holiday with ruthless efficiency, as they cross the River Bosphorus from Europe to Asia, comparing a private yacht tour with the low-price public ferry. The pair have an authentic Turkish shave at a local barbershop that turns out to be a lot closer than they were expecting, and share an awkward moment at a local bath and massage parlour. There's also a visit to the Grand Bazaar market, where both men have a go at haggling

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef continues his exploration of the Far East's most diverse food cultures, travelling down the Mekong River to Vietnam, where the local diet features an abundance of herbs, freshwater fish and flavoursome soups. As well as following in the footsteps of his hero Graham Greene in Saigon, he is reunited with a former employee in Hanoi to try out the capital city's street food, and samples the produce on offer at the night-time stalls in Bangkok, Thailand

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. Richard Hammond explores an alternative to Germanic sports saloons and tests the Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst, and former US chat show host Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell is joined by MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and actor Nigel Havers, while Lee Mack teams up with writer and comedian Charlie Brooker and actress Nina Wadia, as the comedy panel show hosted by Rob Brydon continues

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell is joined by comic actor Greg Davies and TV presenter Konnie Huq, while Lee Mack teams up with former cricketer Phil Tufnell and comedian Marcus Brigstocke, as the comedy panel show hosted by Rob Brydon continues

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer Jordan Stephens, actor Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty test their general knowledge

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade takes actress and writer Jessica Hynes on a whirlwind weekend away to Iceland, a country famous for spectacular water features, geo-thermic pools and a curious belief in elves - all attributes that the pair investigate with ruthless efficiency. They go on the popular Golden Circle Tour of the Nordic island's most famous waterfalls, glaciers and geysers - by helicopter, saving seven valuable hours. They also eat rotten shark and Brenavin, a spirit made from mashed potato, and attend elf school, followed by a speedy boat trip in search of whales. There's also a rapid chill-out at the Blue Lagoon geothermic pool and spa

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedy actor Stephen Mangan for a weekend in the Moroccan city. Wanting to experience its famous street food, the pair sample a local delicacy of steamed sheep's head - an eye-opening culinary experience for them both. They then take a hot-air balloon flight over the Atlas Mountains with breathtaking views, watch a traditional story-telling performance and visit the historic Saadian tombs, as well as Marrakech's ancient tanneries. Finally, in search of a suitable place to sum up the holiday experience, Richard and Stephen head into the desert for a contemplative camel ride

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: David Mitchell, Noel Fielding and Holly Walsh join Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme of 'night' in an extended edition of the panel show. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: It's the first Saturday of the month and Kurtan tries to make a pilgrimage to Swindon to buy the new clothes of the season. However, engineering works on the bus route and a lack of alternative transport options disrupt his plans. Meanwhile, Kerry helps the vicar run the local football practice. Cotswolds-based comedy, written by and starring Daisy May Cooper and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The vicar's son returns to the village and has to readjust to rural life having lived in Bristol, while Kerry tries to give back to the community by visiting an elderly neighbour whose cat has recently passed away

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In an episode originally shown in 2011, Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th anniversary of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern-day interpretation of the classic sports car, and throws a typically low-key party involving live music, fighter planes and the Royal Marines. Richard Hammond is in South Africa, where he tests the Marauder, a bigger, more rugged alternative to the Humvee, and James May enlists the help of 2010 Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams and former Intercontinental Rally Challenge champion Kris Meeke to try out a Mini rally car. programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by Greg Davies for two days in Moscow. The pair's first port of call in the Russian capital is Red Square, where their appreciation of the Kremlin, St Basil's and Lenin's tomb is rudely interrupted as the tanks roll by at rehearsals for a military parade. They also visit the Cosmonaut Museum and try genuine space food, followed by a visit to one of the strangest circuses in the world

programme.title: Red Dwarf

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly's new invention hurls the crew into a parallel universe inhabited by their opposites, confirming Lister's darkest fears, and giving Cat a major shock when he meets his counterpart. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Danny John-Jules and Norman Lovett, boasting a spectacular song-and-dance routine performed by the cast

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: Artefacts for sale at auction are examined to reveal their historical significance, including items linked to German warships, vampire mythology, and Queen Victoria's coronation

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: Auction lots are examined to reveal their historical significance, including a large collection of antique taxidermy and a love token from a convict transported overseas

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the secretive Rosicrucian Order, whose members included Leonardo Da Vinci and Isaac Newton

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitler's ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leader's promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Antiques Roadshow pays tribute to Her Majesty the Queen as she becomes Britain's longest-serving monarch

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring ship graveyards, including Scapa Flow in Orkney, where German warships were scuttled after the First World War, and Mallows Bay, home to 200 wrecks colonised by wildlife

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, travelling from London Bridge through Kent and around the scenic south coast to Hastings. He begins by visiting the Royal Observatory to learn how Greenwich Mean Time standardised time on the railways, goes through the world's first underwater tunnel at Rotherhithe and explores the historic dockyards at Chatham

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey from London Bridge to Hastings, exploring the lives of Victorian hop-pickers along the way. He also examines the history of Maidstone's paper industry, and discovers the role railways played in transforming cricket into a national sport

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an episode from RAF Coningsby, an operational air base in Lincolnshire, where she takes to the sky in a Dakota aircraft and examines some air force memorabilia including a teddy bear that was on board one of the Dambuster planes and designs for the Vulcan bomber. Items brought in to be valued include a painting by John Singer Sergeant, a crystal decanter and an officer's coat from the American Civil War

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of admiring Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his immaculate 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris sets out to reach the northernmost railway station on Earth, inside Russia's Arctic Circle. This is an epic 2,000-mile adventure from Moscow into Arctic Siberia, and begins with a marathon 19-hour leg. Along the way he enjoys a reindeer stew with a local family and takes the controls of one of the massive locomotives that power the trains, before pressing on along railways built under the brutal regime of Joseph Stalin

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: How Europe's most advanced power station became a film set. Plus, the structures made by scientific pioneer Nikola Tesla in his quest to provide the US with free electricity

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: A Scottish soldier who walked 200 miles through the Libyan desert without water or supplies recalls Ronald Moore, the leader of his party

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through Kent. He starts in Canterbury, where he finds out how the city's cathedral was saved from the Second World War air raids, then goes whelk fishing in Whitstable and explores the origins of a seaside swim in Margate

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Sandwich to Folkestone, exploring a secret port that helped ferry troops and vital supplies to France in the First World War. He also visits Walmer Castle, once home to hero of Waterloo the Duke of Wellington, and discovers how the Victorians initiated the building of the Channel Tunnel

programme.title: David Jason: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
programme.desc: The actor ends his journey by learning to drive a stunt car, sampling Hollywood luxury in an executive jet, cruising in a 1950s hot rod and taking an aerial tour

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Harris accidentally drops stolen pills into Fletcher's midday cuppa - much to the annoyance of prison racketeer Harry Grout, who resents a newcomer muscling in on his patch. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher objects to having a third cellmate foisted on him, especially as the newcomer seems to enjoy special privileges and the full backing of the wardens. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates are shocked to discover there is a thief in their midst, so Fletcher decides to stage a mock trial to bring the culprit to justice with the help of Slade's resident legal consultant Judge Rawley. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Maurice Denham

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright attends a former customer's funeral, leaving Granville in charge of the store. However, the miserly shopkeeper has difficulty trusting his nephew to run it properly

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Miserly shopkeeper Arkwright organises a sales drive in a bid to boost profits - and rid himself of the vast consignment of apples he has mistakenly ordered. An unhappy Granville is persuaded to demonstrate the merchandise. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Nurse Gladys Emmanuel's washing-machine breaks down, and Arkwright decides to try and lure her into his home with the offer of his vintage machine

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: A Scottish soldier who walked 200 miles through the Libyan desert without water or supplies recalls Ronald Moore, the leader of his party

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: Featuring items belonging to the crew of the Titanic and the Great Train Robbery suspects, plus dresses worn by Bjork and a key belonging to Catherine the Great

programme.title: Trading History
programme.desc: Featuring cycling jerseys worn by Britain's first Tour de France winner, a rare watch, and a silver cigarette case linked to the Russian Revolution

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston journeys to Greece and Italy to uncover the truth about oracles - mythical figures who could reputedly hear and speak to gods

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an episode from RAF Coningsby, an operational air base in Lincolnshire, where she takes to the sky in a Dakota aircraft and examines some air force memorabilia including a teddy bear that was on board one of the Dambuster planes and designs for the Vulcan bomber. Items brought in to be valued include a painting by John Singer Sergeant, a crystal decanter and an officer's coat from the American Civil War

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: How Europe's most advanced power station became a film set. Plus, the structures made by scientific pioneer Nikola Tesla in his quest to provide the US with free electricity

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through Kent. He starts in Canterbury, where he finds out how the city's cathedral was saved from the Second World War air raids, then goes whelk fishing in Whitstable and explores the origins of a seaside swim in Margate

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Sandwich to Folkestone, exploring a secret port that helped ferry troops and vital supplies to France in the First World War. He also visits Walmer Castle, once home to hero of Waterloo the Duke of Wellington, and discovers how the Victorians initiated the building of the Channel Tunnel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Broughton Castle, near Banbury, Oxfordshire. Among the finds brought in to be valued are an art nouveau vase, a Georgian dolls' house, a Victorian baby bouncer, a letter from Lord Nelson, a book of early police mug shots, a collection of talismans and a musical penknife. Plus, Philip Mould meets a tax inspector who was paid in paintings by several artists

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a Bradford-built 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Paul and Dave uncover a Ford Escort that's been untouched for 30 years

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris attempts to cross the length of Cuba, the only island in the Caribbean to boast an extensive railway network, starting in Havana in the west. This proves to be a major challenge given the unreliability of the service and the old trains regularly used, but makes for a fascinating journey to Chris's ultimate goal, the American naval base at Guantanamo Bay. Along the way he soaks up the sights and sounds of Havana, retraces the footsteps of the country's national hero Che Guevara, and visits the ghost town of Camilo Cienfuegos - a relic from Cuba's colonial sugar plantation era

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The stories behind closed roads, including a route once used by the French Resistance and areas made impassable by crumbling hills and sand dunes

programme.desc: A profile of Dudley Mason, who was awarded the George Cross after the tanker he was commanding came under attack during Operation Pedestal in August 1942 to relieve Malta

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels along England's south coast from Hythe to Hastings. Along the way, he discovers a hardy breed of sheep on Romney Marsh, explores Kent's sparkling-wine industry and learns why the Victorians became obsessed with ferns

programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a journey from Ayr to Skye. He begins by visiting the home town of poet Robert Burns and finds out how to make haggis. programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his late father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris journeys a thousand miles across Japan, starting out in Nagasaki, where the story of eastern railways began 150 years ago with the help of an enterprising Brit. In Fukuoka, he visits one of the Bullet Train's giant repair centres and in Hiroshima, he unravels what happened to its streetcar network after the world's first atomic bomb devastated the city. Along the way, Chris also meets a superstar cat and a singing conductor, while also checking into a popular trainspotter hotel and riding a steam-powered mountain railway

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A fuel plant in Poland built to make Nazi Germany self-sufficient by using the latest technology of the time to turn coal into oil

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: An examination of how Leon Degrelle and an array of Finns, Estonians and fellow Belgians joined German volunteer brigades to hold off the Red Army on the Eastern front during the Second World War and enabled 56,000 men to escape

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey along the west coast of Scotland from Ayr to Skye, stopping off to explore the Dumbarton shipyards that were responsible for the construction of the famous Cutty Sark. He also goes hunting for gold in the mountains, and visits Loch Lomond, one of Queen Victoria's favourite haunts

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey up the west coast of Scotland and discovers how trains helped spread the reputation of Oban whisky. He also learns about the efforts to build a railway through the desolate Rannoch Moor and visits Corrour, a favourite shooting estate of the political elite in Victorian times

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A two-hour special featuring a deserted Caribbean city, an American ghost town still burning and an Arctic settlement facing a terrifying future

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitler's ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leader's promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II. He sailed in from Holland around Lyme's famous Cobb with a handful of soldiers, believing the people would support him in overthrowing the newly crowned James II, who was also Monmouth's uncle, but, more importantly, Roman Catholic. The year was 1685 and the country was riven by anti-Catholic feeling mixed up with revolutionary zeal. Monmouth marched through the fiercely independent towns of Devon, gathering support from artisans and farmworkers. Meeting up with Billy Bragg in Colyton, Tony learns about the kind of people prepared to risk their lives for the Monmouth Rebellion

programme.title: Devonport: Inside the Royal Navy
programme.desc: HMS Bulwark returns from a vital humanitarian mission rescuing migrants in the Mediterranean, and a Devonport sailor takes his relaxation time to excessive new levels

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Revealing how Macarons, Pine Needle Baskets and Micrometers are made, and how the processes have been prefected over time

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The manufacture of endoscopes and megaphones, and the refinement of uranium

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Surrey, five cooks from the Woking and Weybridge area battle to win the culinary contest's £1,000 prize. The first to host is Vincenzo Ferlita, who works in an ice-cream van. Staying true to his Italian and Sicilian roots, he hopes that his wealth of family recipes will impress his guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Canadian housewife and craft-maker Sarah Maynard is second to play host in the Woking and Weybridge area, and hopes to impress her guests by serving up a three-course Turkish feast. However, the lavish spread needs a lot of preparation time, and she must hope it turns out as presentable as she plans it to be

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Riot policeman Steve Carvill hosts the third dinner party in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and hopes that a night of fine food and fun will impress his guests. He takes no prisoners with his menu, rustling up home-made pasta and white chocolate panna cotta from scratch for his four unsuspecting diners, who are expecting fast food rather than fine dining

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of gardener Sara Gilding on the fourth night in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, but it is her first time hosting a dinner party in more than 20 years. Guest Vincenzo Ferlita is not looking forward to the evening, while Steve Carvill hopes he will get some respite from Sara's flirtatious advances

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Children's entertainer Kelly Cummins hosts the final party from Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced. The guests keep their fingers crossed that dog-loving Sara Gilding does not return to her critical ways, while a bout of flatulence provides some comic relief

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Best friends for over 20 years, Julie and Jan from Tyne and Wear have had several holidays on the island of Lanzarote. They're now dreaming of owning a bolthole on the island to enjoy with their friends and families and have a budget of £115,000 between them. Jasmine Harman joins them on the Canary Island and shows them five properties to consider, all around the Puerto del Carmen area

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: John and Della Baines' daughter Megan has already moved to Cyprus. After visiting her and seeing the island for themselves, John and Della have decided it was the perfect spot for a holiday home. For their budget of £45,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five value apartments in and around the west coast resort of Paphos

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Guest House Worsthorne in Lancashire, where partners Dave Rogers and Sanchia Crossley aim to win over their guests

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Retirees from Cheltenham want a property in Almeria to accommodate as many of their 17 grandkids as possible, while a couple from Derby would like a three-bedroom villa in Alicante

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A cancelled date leaves Niles, Martin and Daphne suspecting Frasier's supermodel escort is just a figment of his fevered imagination - so he tries to provide photographic evidence of the relationship. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier go gift-shopping with a vengeance as Martin's 65th birthday approaches, vying with each other to be the apple of their father's eye on his big day. Starring John Mahoney, Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his visit to Zayna Flaming Grill in Redondo Beach, California, and finds more questions than answers as he tries to save the crisis-ridden restaurant from disaster. While trying to inject some much-needed flavour into the tired Mediterranean menu, the chef also encourages aunt-and-niece duo Faye and Brenda to settle their differences

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman attempts to find a dream home in Barbados for husband and wife Julie and John, who have a budget of £300,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Wiltshire mother and daughter Emma and Charlotte have a budget of £100,000 for a holiday home in Antequera in Spain where they hope to spend more quality time together as a family. Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops to show five suitable properties

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eatery's failings. Their 78-year-old mother Inge desperate for Nino to turn the business around, but the feuding family members will have to solve their differences first

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge use the handiwork and design skills with which they have renovated their French chateau to give viewers ideas on fixing up their own homes. In the first show, Angel helps Miriam transform her 1970s bathroom into a relaxing sanctuary - without changing either the suite or the tiles - and Dick digs out his old handmade smoker to help teach Phil the ancient art of cheese smoking. The couple also advise Tom and Sim how to bring their unique front door into the 21st century

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Identical twins Nik and Jon Daughtry build two near-identical houses next door to each other in Sheffield, paying tribute to the city's industrial past. Their plans are incredibly ambitious, not only do they want to build the two houses for the price of one, but the materials and finish have to be absolutely perfect. With a tight budget, it's an almighty stretch from the start. As delays, overages and imperfections hit, it's clear the twins' intuitive and incredibly close relationship will be tested like never before

programme.title: Guy Martin: World's Fastest Tractor
programme.desc: Guy embarks on his latest speed challenge, as he tries to become the first person to drive a tractor at more than 100mph, at Elvington Airfield near York. He also has a go at the sport of tractor pulling and helps with designing, welding, putting the engine together and testing tyres on his new souped-up tractor, before aiming to write himself into the record books

programme.title: Travel Man's Greatest Trips
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade raids the Travel Man archive to look back on some of his greatest trips. In this episode, he presides over a selection of the finest adventures from over 40 mini-breaks, beginning with a tour of Vienna in miniature hot rods with Chris O'Dowd, before jetting off to Dubai with comedian Johnny Vegas to scale the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa. He also recalls canoeing around Helsinki's 300 islands with Paul Rudd, rafting in Florence with Rebel Wilson and sightseeing in Iceland with Jessica Hynes

programme.title: The Talk
programme.desc: Famous parents of black and mixed race children share their personal stories of making them aware of racism, and being given similar lessons by their own parents. Tinie Tempah, Emeli Sandé, Leigh-Anne Pinnock, Lennie James, Maro Itoje and Ade Adepitan are among the contributors discussing being on both sides of this commonplace exchange, and how they dealt with subjects like being seen as different or dealing with the police

programme.title: Churchill's Secret Affair
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the secret relationship between the prime minister and a woman he met in the South of France in the 1930s. During an important visit to President Roosevelt at the height of the war, he was reunited with his former lover, now stranded in the US and in possession of a portrait that could have a devastating impact during a dangerous period for the nation

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more singletons to choose a date after seeing what their potential partners have to offer on a purely physical level. First up is 25-year-old Hope, a model from Birmingham. A huge wrestling fan, she is keen to find a man who can finally pin her down. She is followed by 28-year-old veterinary student Kurt, a real-life Dr Doolittle who can talk to the animals but gets tongue-tied speaking to women. Will Naked Attraction's unique dating method help him overcome his shyness?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shania and Farrukh are on the hunt for the perfect family property in Florida. Ben Hillman helps by lining up five options, hoping one will become their dream holiday home

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dairy farmer's wife and Disney enthusiast Charlene Fry-Henderson hosts the first dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes that her locally reared meat, home cooking, and entertainment involving a plastic cow will secure her the £1,000 prize. Her guests are local radio presenter Tommy Jardine, banker Olive Melville, prison custody officer Tammy Rice and trainee teacher Josh Jones

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast and banker Olive Melville hosts the second dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, where she hopes to impress her guests with a crab starter and mousse dessert - but things take a turn for the worse when a conversation about the royal family divides the diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Local radio DJ Tommy Jardine hosts the third dinner party in Dumfries and Galloway, and prepares chicken stuffed with haggis - but first he must continue a family tradition and rustle up a sheep for his entertainment. Tommy then tries to impress his guests with a touch of celebrity name-dropping, but young dance DJ and trainee teacher Josh trumps him at his own game

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee teacher and club DJ Josh Jones hosts the fourth night in Dumfries and Galloway, and hopes he can broaden his fellow contestants' palates with his menu of Indian food. He plans lamb pasanda and tandoori monkfish, but Tammy Rice finds something in her glass that wasn't on the menu. However, Josh hopes his disco entertainment might impress the guests enough to bag him the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tammy Rice hosts the final evening in Dumfries and Galloway, hoping to win the £1,000 prize with her no-nonsense approach and traditional Scottish fare. The dinner-party novice rustles up chorizo and cous cous stuffed peppers for her starter and incorporates haggis into her chicken-based main course

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Wiltshire mother and daughter Emma and Charlotte have a budget of £100,000 for a holiday home in Antequera in Spain where they hope to spend more quality time together as a family. Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops to show five suitable properties

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Dentist Michelle Naik and retired entrepreneur Marshal Sutherland from Sheffield have fallen in love with the Caribbean island of Aruba. The couple have now decided it's the perfect place to find an island retreat. With a budget of £130,000 and the help of Scarlette Douglas, they hope to find a holiday home that could one day become a permanent retirement location

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week, the group heads to the Sleeperdorm Backpacker Hotel in Newcastle, where Ian Nicholls hopes his guests will enjoy his cheap and cheerful concept

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners' next customer in Almería is 76-year-old Sylvia, from Worcestershire, who had planned on moving to Spain with her husband until he died a few years ago. It's Sharon and Steve's job to help her fulfil her dream, but Sylvia is a woman who knows exactly what she wants, and that includes a single-storey property, close to amenities, with a large kitchen, separate dining room, and cream floor tiles. In Alicante, the Rodriguez family looks for a permanent home for lovebirds Adele and Phil. For this green-fingered couple, outside space is a top priority for their 250,000 euro budget

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. Niles throws a fancy-dress Halloween party with a literary theme as a benefit for the Library Association, which he attends dressed as Cyrano de Bergerac, while a misunderstanding lets one or two skeletons out of the closet. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. Roz panics at the thought of becoming a mother, prompting Frasier to lend her a friendly shoulder to cry on - while he ponders the father's identity. Award-winning comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Rosaria bought Bella Luna to realise her son Gianfranco's ambition to become a chef, but the Italian restaurant's future looks bleak due to his lack of experience in the kitchen combined with her refusal to acknowledge the eatery's many flaws. Gordon Ramsay heads to Easton, Pennsylvania, to help the troubled pair and save the business from financial disaster

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows five properties to recently married Londoners Katy and Dom, who want to buy a holiday home in the Cadiz area of south-west Spain. With a budget of up to £180,000, will any of the five properties Jasmine has lined up to show them be their perfect place in the sun?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shania and Farrukh are on the hunt for the perfect family property in Florida. Ben Hillman helps by lining up five options, hoping one will become their dream holiday home

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across America saving failing restaurants from disaster. In the first of two visits to Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, the chef encounters Joe Nagy, who claims he has the best menu from New York to Los Angeles. However, his servers and diners tell a different story, and his resistance to change leads to some explosive confrontations

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and Dr Helen Lawal challenge three pairs of friends to try out the latest popular diets, while cook Stacie Stewart demonstrates some healthy recipes. Sophie and Zoe give the Citrus Diet and the Sexy Pineapple Diet a road test, Barbara and Denise spend six weeks on the Summer Salad Diet and the Summer Sandwich Diet ahead of their holiday in Ibiza, and Theo and Ervis take on the Greek Island Longevity Diet and the South American Diet so they can lose weight in time for a pool party

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Fisherman Stephen Perham prepares for the lucrative Clovelly Lobster Festival, while Torquay harbourmaster Nick Burns awaits the world's largest residential cruise ship

programme.title: Race Against the Virus: The Hunt for a Vaccine
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Covid-19 spread unstoppably throughout the world, told through the eyes of key scientists on the pandemic frontline. Personal diaries recorded by scientists and intensive care doctors give an insight into the race to identify the virus and try to unlock the secrets of its virulence, revealing how scientists came to realise the seriousness of the disease. Racing to develop a vaccine, leading scientist Professor Sarah Gilbert discusses the pressures she faces and her hopes that a vaccine can be developed

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Doctor Michael Mosley puts five volunteers on a low-calorie diet to help get their health back on track after the UK's Covid-19 lockdown. In the first episode, Michael runs a series of tests to find out just how badly lockdown has affected his volunteers' health, before creating a bespoke diet plan for the 21 days. He also tests misconceptions of what a healthy BMI looks like, and explores the cutting-edge research which indicates ties between BMI and survivability from Covid-19

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Another selection of memorable stories filmed at King's College and St George's hospitals in south London, with this edition focusing on patients and staff who have all overcome personal setbacks and adversity. Eve, 83, has fallen down the stairs into her basement and has an open ankle fracture that needs urgent realignment to restore the blood flow to her leg, while four-year-old Maddi has fallen over and injured her neck on a train

programme.title: Phil Spencer's Stately Homes
programme.desc: The property expert returns to look round more of Britain's stately homes, beginning at Chatsworth House in Derbyshire, the family seat of the Cavendish family and the Dukes of Devonshire for more than 350 years. The 11th Duke of Devonshire, Peregrine Andrew Morney Cavendish, shows Phil around, revealing what it's like to live in such a palatial property

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree host their last wedding of the year, and they welcome a fleet of vintage Citroens, whose drivers are making a pit-stop on a charity tour

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Having holidayed many times in Torrox Costa, Spain, house-hunter Judith now feels the time has arrived for her to realise her dream of finding a holiday home in the region. Laura Hamilton pulls out all the stops to show five properties within her budget of £100,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Angie and Neelesh met 30 years ago on the London Underground, and now with retirement on the horizon they think the time is right to buy a holiday home in their favourite Spanish town of Estepona. The couple have their hearts set on a new build property and have asked Laura Hamilton to help find them the perfect one. Laura lines up various options for their maximum budget of £260,000, but will any impress them enough to make an offer?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Divorce lawyer Michelle Camacho hosts the first dinner party in south London, where she hopes that her 1970s menu of duck a l'orange and coleslaw, vegetables and a Bloody Mary sorbet for dessert will help to secure her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pub manager and artist Matt Wren hosts the second evening in south London, but his dinner party is beset by disasters in the kitchen and arguing guests at the dinner table when Michelle and Licia have a spat. However, one of the five takes a liking to another of the contestants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Licia Snow hosts the third party in south London, and hopes to impress her guests with rustic and homely touches during her laid-back evening. However, her relaxed and calm approach could be threatened when she clashes with Michelle at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Raphael Steele hosts the fourth night in south London and hopes that his first-ever dinner party will be a success. He plans a Caribbean evening based on old family recipes featuring ackee, saltfish and curried goat, and organises steel drum music for entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Swedish businesswoman Charlotte Sundberg hosts the final party in south London. Her tactics are to present a night full of schnapps, folk songs and classic cuisine from her Scandinavian homeland, which she hopes will help her scoop the cash prize

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shania and Farrukh are on the hunt for the perfect family property in Florida. Ben Hillman helps by lining up five options, hoping one will become their dream holiday home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Devon couple Alan and Jayne Poole have been holidaying in Orlando for many years and are in love with American culture so much so that they are now ready to splash out on a holiday home in the Sunshine State. With the help of property expert Laura Hamilton and a budget of £115,000, they are hoping to find somewhere that they and their family can enjoy for years to come

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B battle continues at the Parsonage Hotel and Spa near York, a 55-room country manor which wows the guests with its size, although Mandi's high cleaning standards are not met

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Wendy searches for a villa for a couple want to swap Kent winters for the Spanish sunshine, and Chris and Carol looking to swap their motor home for a permanent retirement retreat

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Special episode filmed on location in Seattle. Frasier is guest of honour at a public rally to mark his 1,000th radio show - but the celebrations go awry as Niles's jealousy reaches new peaks. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink is asked to be a guest speaker on an all-star cruise, and invites the family to accompany him. However, the so-called celebrities also appearing are not exactly famous. Meanwhile, Niles's marital problems worsen once he discovers Maris will also be coming along

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the concluding part of his trip to the Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, Gordon's disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet. Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?

programme.title: Gone Girl (2014)
programme.desc: A woman disappears without trace on her fifth wedding anniversary. Her husband contacts the police, but struggles to cope as his marriage is scrutinised by the media. As his behaviour grows stranger and more evasive, he finds himself accused of her murder. David Fincher's crime thriller based on Gillian Flynn's novel, starring Ben Affleck, Rosamund Pike and Neil Patrick Harris

programme.desc: Famous parents of black and mixed race children share their personal stories of making them aware of racism, and being given similar lessons by their own parents. Tinie Tempah, Emeli Sandé, Leigh-Anne Pinnock, Lennie James, Maro Itoje and Ade Adepitan are among the contributors discussing being on both sides of this commonplace exchange, and how they dealt with subjects like being seen as different or dealing with the police

programme.desc: House-hunters Charlotte and Ben are looking to move their young family lock, stock and barrel to the Central American country of Costa Rica. With their £200,000 budget, Ben Hillman shows them five family homes. However, it's a huge life-changing decision, so will any of them be the right fit for the adventurous pair?
